blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: The group. Tell me what you observed at the bonfire beyond what you gave me last night.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Six people. Longstanding group — the body language of people who've moved past performance with each other. Easy. Unguarded. Except it wasn't entirely. Groups like that still have fault lines. History makes the present complicated.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Meaning I walked past that fire for eight seconds and read four distinct emotional states across six people. That's not a relaxed group on vacation. That's a group managing several things at once beneath the surface.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: You got all that in eight seconds.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: I got the outline in eight seconds. I've been filling in the detail since.
Davenport set his pen down. The kind of gesture that means he's deciding something.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: One of my detectives has fifteen years and a criminology degree. She interviewed all five this morning and told me the group seemed genuinely devastated. Nothing stood out.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: She's right. Nothing stands out. That's the point. What you're looking for isn't going to stand out — it's going to look exactly like everything else around it. That's the only way this works.
Davenport looked at him for a long moment.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: What else haven't you told me yet.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: One thing at a time, Inspector. Let's get through the statement first.
